author: @rootieboy
text: I'm a 6th grade English teacher. While we were reading "The Boy Who Harnessed the Wind" I had an old vacuum in my classroom that no longer worked. The kids would take turns reading out loud and when they finished reading their part I would give them a screw driver and let them remove a screw from the vacuum. We did this until we were able to get down to the motor. I showed them the internal workings of the motor and how it was still usable. They are becoming inspired to see other objects they can find that have purpose that would have otherwise been thrown away.

author: @peerperemans897
text:
Since it has no permanent magnets...does it simply start on some remanent magnetism left in the mild steel cores and sweep up from there? Must be...
Since so many ebikes become ewaste I used a direct drive type, alu blades cut from irigation pipe pop-rivetted straight to the rim and the hub, spokes all still in place. Been running for more the 10 years now. Needs furling, limiting, static balancing and a rectifier but fun!

That's what everybody asks looking at my windmill. I wonder if quantifying electricity is within everybody's understanding. 
It's proof of concept, nothing else. Go build stuff, fail and try again.

author: @hanslepoeter5167
text:
Did you ask yourself why it's a generator ? It's not so obvious. Remember there's no permanent magnet in there no' re is there initial current there to turn either stator or rotor into an electromagnet. Why does it start generating without magnetic field in the first place ? It actually starts due to earth magnetic field. In theory it should start with reverse polarity if you rotate it 180 degree. I never tried that but you might.
Anyway, it's a series motor. Generation will depend on magnetic field generated, so current, so the electrical load you connect. Your multimeter 'll be 10 Mohms or so and that's a very small load. If you connect a 200 ohm/100W load or so I think it will produce serious power at high RPM.

author: @protyusgames4741
text: For me the issue with renewable power is the storage of energy.  So my goal is always to pump water up hill, to use its potential when other sources are unavailable.  If you get a bit of time could you help me with that.

replies.author: @garethb5729
replies.text:
Water hammer pump. It will take 10% of the original water supply and pump it uphill. It will even pump vertically. 
And yeah it requires no electronics. Very Very easy to make.

replies.author: @protyusgames4741
replies.text: I m thinking of a pumped hydro system, with a large storage tank at the bottom of my property, and a large storage at the top.  Like 140 ft differential.  I do not have access to The type of stream required for the water hammer.  Wish I did.

replies.author: @garethb5729
